violations.text: The drainage from the grease trap unit is draining into a container. Condensate drainage and other nonsewage liquids must drain into a drain. New Violation. Correct By: 04/08/2026

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. Manager is trained as a Food Safety Manager but is not registered with RIDOH. Establishment must register manager as a certified Rhode Island Food Safety Manager. New Violation. Correct By: 11/15/2025
The milk case located in the cafeteria area was not in good repair. Equipment must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 11/15/2025

The milk cased located in the cafeteria was not in good repair. Equipment must be maintained in working condition. The cold well on the main serving line was observed in poor repair. Equipment must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 04/25/2025

The door to the outside, located in the cafeteria, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 11/03/2024

The area behind the cooking line in the kitchen has an accumulation of soil residue, droppings, and food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 10/05/2024

The doors to the outside, located in the kitchen (receiving) and in the cafeteria, are not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/20/2023

Turbo Air and cold station #1 are not in good repair. Both units are not currently functioning. In addition, milk cold hold #1 (service line) and milk cold hold #2 (cafeteria) have gaskets that need replacing. Equipment must be maintained in good repair. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/20/2023

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. Establishment shall register a ServSafe certified employee with RIDOH Center for Food Protection as the Certified Food Safety Manager (RI license). New Violation. Correct By: 10/31/2023

The doors to the outside, located in the back kitchen area and doors to the outside in the cafeteria, observed not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/31/2023

The cold station on service line 2 and the 1 door Turbo Air refrgerator were not in good repair. Both units were not in use at the time of this inspection. Equipment shall be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 10/20/2023
Refrigerator door gaskets at Dairy chest cooler 4 were observed in poor repair. Ice buoldup observed in unit. Equipment shall be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 10/13/2023

The door to the outside in the back kitchen area and doors to the outside in the cafeteria, observed not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 08/31/2023
The paper towel dispenser observed over the food prep table. Paper towel dispenser shall be moved away from the food prep area. New Violation. Correct By: 08/31/2023

The Person in charge did not assure compliance with critical code requirements. Management's plan for assessing, monitoring, and controlling foodborne disease hazards is inadequate. New Violation. Correct By: 04/20/2023

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. New Violation. Correct By: 04/20/2023

The sanitizer concentration in the sanitizing solution of the 3-bay sink was not at concentration levels required for sanitizing. The concentration of sanitizing chemicals must be followed according to the manufacturer's use directions. District manager was notified, who will place a service request to adjust sanitizer levels. Sanitizer was manually adjusted during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/20/2023

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Chicken nuggets for salads were placed hot in the refrigerator in covered containers greater than four inches deep. Chicken nuggets were placed in a single layer in shallow pans and placed in the freezer to rapidly cool. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/20/2023
